- ## Overview
- ### Architecture Components
- **LINSTOR Controller**: Central management component
- **LINSTOR Satellites**: Node-level storage managers
- **DRBD**: Kernel module for block replication
- **CSI Driver**: Kubernetes storage interface
- **Piraeus Operator**: Kubernetes operator for lifecycle management
- ## Prerequisites
- ### Hardware Requirements
- Minimum 3 worker nodes for redundancy
- Dedicated storage devices (NVMe/SSD recommended)
- 10GbE+ network for storage traffic (recommended)
- 8GB+ RAM per node (16GB+ recommended)
- ### Software Requirements
- Kubernetes 1.21+ cluster
- Linux kernel 4.14+ (5.x recommended)
- kubectl CLI tool
- helm 3.x (optional)
- ### Network Requirements
- Nodes must be able to communicate on ports:
- 3366-3370 (LINSTOR)
- 7000-7999 (DRBD)
- 9942-9943 (Prometheus metrics)

- ### 4. Performance Tuning
- ## Best Practices
- **Resource Planning**
- Plan for 20-30% overhead in storage pools
- Use thin provisioning for better utilization
- Monitor pool usage regularly
- **Network Configuration**
- Use dedicated network for storage traffic
- Enable jumbo frames (MTU 9000) if possible
- Consider RDMA for ultra-low latency
- **Security**
- Enable encryption for sensitive data
- Use NetworkPolicies to restrict traffic
- Regular security updates
- **Disaster Recovery**
- Regular backups of LINSTOR configuration
- Test restore procedures
- Document runbooks
- **Monitoring**
- Set up alerts for pool utilization
- Monitor DRBD sync status
- Track performance metrics
- ## Conclusion
- Proper node preparation is crucial
- Start simple and add complexity gradually
- Monitor and tune based on workload requirements
- Regular maintenance prevents issues
- [LINSTOR Documentation](https://linbit.com/drbd-user-guide/linstor-guide-1_0-en/)
